summary: Lead Aging + SLA Push Notifications Feature - Full Testing Complete. Backend: 14/14 tests passed. Frontend: All notification features working - notification bell with unread badge, dropdown with SLA breach list, severity badges (WARN/BREACH), mark as read, mark all read, admin SLA alert banner, view link navigation.

issues: Console hydration warnings - span inside tbody/tr elements (not critical, cosmetic issue from React's visual edits wrapper)

critical_code_review_comments: Backend notifications.py: Clean implementation with proper auth dependency, unread_count in response
Backend sla_monitor_service.py: Well-structured with deduplication (6h window), multi-category alerts (first_contact, stage_aging, hold_expiry, payment_pending), both RM and admin notifications
Backend admin.py trigger-sla-check: Properly protected with require_role('admin')
Frontend NotificationBell.js: 30-second polling, severity-based styling (critical=red, warning=amber), data-testid attributes present
Frontend ControlRoom.js: SLA alert banner with breach count, severity breakdown, affected lead names
Server.py: Background SLA monitor loop runs every 15 minutes, started on app startup
seed_data_creation: SLA test data was seeded by main agent: 3 leads (Priya Patel, Raj Mehta, Ananya Singh) with SLA breaches
context_for_next_testing_agent: SLA Notifications feature fully implemented and tested. Key collections: notifications (type='sla_breach' for SLA alerts), sla_alerts_log (dedup tracking), audit_logs (breach entries). Backend APIs: GET /api/notifications, PUT /api/notifications/{id}/read, PUT /api/notifications/read-all, POST /api/admin/trigger-sla-check (admin only), GET /api/admin/sla-breaches (admin only). Frontend: NotificationBell.js polls every 30s, shows severity badges. ControlRoom.js shows SLA alert banner with breach summary. Deduplication prevents duplicate alerts within 6-hour window. Background SLA monitor runs every 15 minutes.

features_tested: SLA notifications show WARN/BREACH badges with color coding
Shows breach count (e.g., '4 SLA Breaches Detected')
Shows severity breakdown (X critical, Y warnings)
Shows affected lead names with stage
Color-coded: red for critical, amber for warnings

deduplication.behavior: Second SLA check returns 0 new alerts within 6-hour window
